question: In segmentation, each address is specified by ____________
In paging the user provides only ________ which is partitioned by the hardware into ________ and ______
Each entry in a segment table has a ____________

options.a: starting logical address of the process
options.b: starting physical address of the segment in memory

options.a: starting logical address of the process
options.b: starting physical address of the segment in memory

question: The offset ‘d’ of the logical address must be ____________

options.a: it is used as a physical memory address itself
options.b: it is subtracted from the segment base to produce the physical memory address
options.c: it is added to the segment base to produce the physical memory address

question: When the entries in the segment tables of two different processes point to the same physical location ____________
The protection bit is 0/1 based on ____________
If there are 32 segments, each of size 1Kb, then the logical address should have ____________
explanation: To specify a particular segment, 5 bits are required. To select a particular byte after selecting a page, 10 more bits are required. Hence 15 bits are required.

question: Consider a computer with 8 Mbytes of main memory and a 128K cache. The cache block size is 4 K. It uses a direct mapping scheme for cache management. How many different main memory blocks can map onto a given physical cache block?
A multilevel page table is preferred in comparison to a single level page table for translating virtual address to physical address because ____________

options.a: it reduces the memory access time to read or write a memory location
options.b: it helps to reduce the size of page table needed to implement the virtual address space of a process
options.c: it is required by the translation lookaside buffer
options.d: it helps to reduce the number of page faults in page replacement algorithms
